State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901               322-6441              322-6444

January 25, 1985


Robert B. Yee
1440 Broadway, Suite 810
Oakland, CA 94612

                                          Re: Your Request for Advice
                                          Our File No. A-84-323

Dear Mr. Yee:

     Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Tony
Leong concerning his duties under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/  This advice is
based on the facts provided in your letter.

                                    FACTS

     Dr. Leong is an employee of the Berkeley Department of
Health and Human Services. Specifically, Dr. Leong is a Program
Supervisor for the Family, Youth and Children's Program of the
Mental Health Division of the Department of Health and Human
Services. Dr. Leong's position of Program Supervisor is
described in the City job announcement as having "extensive
management, administrative and supervisory responsibilities,
including authority for making and recommending policy
decisions." Responsibilities include "program planning,
implementation, development and evaluation; determining
operations, activities, and organization of program . . .
preparing reports, proposals and budget requests . . . working
with other community resources and agencies to develop an
effective and responsive program."

     Dr. Leong is also the Executive Director of Asians for Job
Opportunities in Berkeley (AJOB), a nonprofit community agency
which receives funding from the City of Berkeley, among other

_______________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

Robert B. Yee
January 25, 1985
Page 2


sources, for counseling, advocacy, job placement, job
development, English as a second language, and other services
for Asians. Dr. Leong receives a salary from AJOB in excess of
$100 per month. As Executive Director, he prepares proposals
for funding, handles personnel matters, and oversees the budget
of the community agency.

AJOB submits annual funding proposals to the City of
Berkeley. The City Health and Human Services Department reviews
proposals for certain types of funds allocated to community
agencies such as AJOB. Specifically, the Department reviews
proposals for funds under the Joint Training and Partnership Act
and Community Services Block Grant programs.

When a community agency submits a proposal for funds,
employees of the Health and Human Services Department with
expertise in the area covered by the proposal review the
proposal. After the review, the City Manager recommends to the
City Council a decision on the allocation of grant funds to the
community agency. If the City Council makes the allocation,
Health and Human Services Department employees negotiate with
the representatives of the funded agency to develop the final
program and prepare and execute a contract. The Department
staff also reviews and approves invoices for payment and
conducts an annual performance evaluation.

As Executive Director of AJOB, Dr. Leong prepares the AJOB
proposals for funds, advocates these proposals before the City
Council, City Manager, and other City employees, negotiates and
signs the contracts, and submits AJOB's invoices to the City for
payment.

As a Program Supervisor in the Health and Human Services
Department, Dr. Leong does not review proposals for funding
under the Joint Training and Partnership Act or the Community
Services Block Grant program. Another unit in the Health and
Human Services Department conducts the review. However, you
have stated that a person in Dr. Leong's position might review
grant proposals for programs of the type provided by AJOB,
although Dr. Leong does not currently review, and has not been
asked to review, any of those proposals.

Dr. Leong is involved in determining City policies and
priorities for mental health problems for youth, which may
include issues related to job training for youth, one of the
issues addressed in programs provided by AJOB.

AJOB also receives Community Development Block Grant
funds. These funds are administered by the Planning and

Robert B. Yee
January 25, 1985
Page 3


Community Development Department, not the Health and Human
Services Department. Dr. Leong does not have any professional
contact or interaction with City staff who review Community
Development Block Grant proposals or administer the Community
Development Block Grant program. Dr. Leong has taken the same
actions with respect to preparing AJOB's grant application and
obtaining Community Development Block Grant funds as are
outlined for Joint Training and Partnership Act and Community
Services Block Grant funds.

AJOB also applies for funds which are not directly
administered by the City, although the City has certain duties
in the application procedure for these funds. For example,
several months ago AJOB submitted a grant proposal for state
mental health funds to provide mental health counseling services
to the Asian adult immigrant population. Although these funds
are not administered by the City, the City is required to sign
off on applications submitted by community agencies. Dr. Leong
prepared and signed AJOB's application and communicated directly
with his supervisor to obtain his approval of AJOB's
application.

The City has also applied for the state mental health
funds, which it proposes to use to operate Health and Human
Services Department programs. As Program Supervisor of the
Family, Youth and Children's unit, Dr. Leong helped to prepare
the City's application and will be responsible for administering
these funds if they are received.

QUESTIONS

Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant
proposals are processed by the Health and Human Services
Department? This question concerns Joint Training and
Partnership Act and Community Services Block Grant funds.

Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant
proposals are processed by the Planning and Community
Development Department? This question concerns Community
Development Block Grant funds.

Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to
grants administered by state agencies, but for which the City
must approve the proposal or take some other action regarding
the proposal?

Robert B. Yee
January 25, 1985
Page 4


DISCUSSION

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making or
participating in a governmental decision in which he knows or
has reason to know he has a financial interest.

Section 87103 provides that an official has a financial
interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on, among other interests, any source of income
aggregating $250 or more received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made.

Dr. Leong receives a salary in excess of $100 per month
from AJOB; therefore AJOB is a source of income in excess of
$250 to Dr. Leong.

The Commission, by regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702, has defined several ways in which a decision can have a
material financial effect on a source of income. A decision is
considered to have a "material" effect on a nonprofit
organization if the decision's effect will be "significant."
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section18702(b)(3)(D).) A determination as to
whether an effect is "significant" can only be made in the
context of a specific situation, after considering all the
facts. A decision whether to make a grant to a nonprofit
corporation is likely to be the type of decision which would
have a significant effect on the nonprofit corporation. As
stated before, whether a decision regarding a grant would have a
significant effect on AJOB depends on the specific facts of the
decision. However, it appears that, in most instances,
Dr. Leong must refrain from making, participating in, or using
his official position to influence, decisions regarding grants
for AJOB.2/

In addition, under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(B),
a decision has a material financial effect on an official's
source of income when "there is a nexus between a governmental
decision and the purpose for which the official receives
income...." This regulation, in effect, prevents Dr. Leong from
accomplishing as a City employee what he is paid to do as
Executive Director of AJOB.

_______________________

2/ Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700
defines making, participating in, and using one's official
position to influence a governmental decision (copy enclosed).

Robert B. Yee
January 25, 1985
Page 5


Since part of Dr. Leong's duties as Executive Director of
AJOB is to prepare grant proposals and advocate the funding of
those proposals before the City, there would be a nexus between
Dr. Leong's position as Executive Director of AJOB and any
actions Dr. Leong would take as a City employee which could
foreseeably further the allocation of grant funds to AJOB.
Accordingly, Dr. Leong, as an employee of the City, is required
to refrain from making, participating in, or using his official
position to influence decisions which could foreseeably affect
grant allocations to AJOB.

Having outlined the general restrictions on Dr. Leong's
participation in decisions affecting AJOB, I shall now apply
these general principles to the specific facts presented.

With regard to your first question, which concerns grant
funds allocated pursuant to the Joint Training and Partnership
Act and Community Block Services Grant programs and administered
by the Health and Human Services Department, Dr. Leong must
refrain from participating in the review or approval process for
any grant proposals submitted by AJOB or submitted by other
agencies competing with AJOB for those funds. Dr. Leong must
also refrain from participating in the negotiation or
administration of contracts between the City and AJOB in
connection with those grant funds, on behalf of either the City
or AJOB. You have stated that Dr. Leong's current
responsibilities as Program Supervisor do not include review
approval, negotiation, or administration of these contracts;
however, I have included this information so that Dr. Leong
would be aware of his responsibilities under the Political
Reform Act in the event of a change in his duties as Program
Supervisor.

As Program Supervisor, Dr. Leong also participates in
establishing policies and priorities for certain mental health
problems which could include issues related to programs provided
by AJOB. With respect to each of these decisions, Dr. Leong
should consider whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
policy or priority decision will materially affect the
allocation of grant funds to AJOB. For example, Dr. Leong must
refrain from participating in any decision to discontinue City
programs under which AJOB currently receives grant funds, or to
lower the funding priority of those programs to the extent that
it is likely that no funds, or a significantly reduced amount of
funds, would be available.

Your second question concerns Community Development Block
Grant funds allocated to AJOB by the Planning and Community
Development Department. Dr. Leong, in his position with the

Robert B. Yee
January 25, 1985
Page 6


Health and Human Services Department, has no involvement in this
grant application review and approval process; therefore,
Dr. Leong has no conflict of interest with respect to these
grant proposals.

Your third question concerns grants from the state or other
sources. Although the City does not administer these funds the
City must approve or otherwise act on the proposals. Dr. Leong
must refrain from participating in the City's actions on any
grant proposals submitted by AJOB, and he must refrain from
representing AJOB to the Department of Health and Human Services
on these grant proposals. He must also refrain from
participating in the City's actions on, or representing AJOB
with regard to, any grant proposals which could foreseeably
affect the allocation of grant funds to AJOB, such as proposals
by other community agencies to provide similar services in the
same area when there is a substantial likelihood that only one
of these agencies will receive funding.

You have stated that if the City receives state mental
health funds for its Family, Youth and Children's program,
Dr. Leong will administer those funds. It is not clear whether
the programs proposed by the City could foreseeably affect
AJOB's ability to receive funding for or to provide services.
However, Dr. Leong must examine each decision regarding the
administration of these City programs, and must refrain from
participating in decisions which could foreseeably have a
significant effect on AJOB's funding or services.

My advice is confined to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act. You should contact the City Attorney's Office
concerning the possible application of Section 1090, which is
outside the Fair Political Practices Commission's jurisdiction.
If you have any further questions regarding the Political Reform
Act, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:plh
Enclosure

LAW OFFICE OF
ROBERT B. YEE

1440 BROADWAY, SUITE 810
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612
(415) 832-5309
832-5309

December 17, 1984

Barbara Milman
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest Involving City Employee who
Serves as Executive Director of a Community Agency
that Receives City Funds

Dear Ms. Milman:

This office represents Tony Leong, Jr., the Executive
Director of Asians for Job Opportunities in Berkeley (AJOB),
in a dispute with the City of Berkeley regarding allegations
of my client's conflict of interest. Upon the recommendation
of the Berkeley City Attorney, this letter requests advice
with regard to the official duties of my client as a City
employee.

He is employed as a Program Supervisor in the Mental
Health Division of Berkeley's Department of Health and Human
Services. At the same time, he is employed as the Executive
Director of a community agency. Questions have arisen
concerning the activities in which Dr. Leong can engage.
While I realize that you do not give advice on past events,
this letter describes past events for illustrative purposes
only, as they are typical of the situations which have arisen
and which may arise in the future.

Dr. Tony Leong has been working for the Mental Health
Division of the City of Berkeley since April, 1979. He was
originally hired on an hourly basis and in January, 1983,
converted to a part-time career status with the City. In
April, 1984, Dr. Leong was hired as Program Supervisor for
the Family, Youth and Children's program in the City's Mental
Health Division. That position is a full-time career
position which is described in the City job announcement as
having "extensive management, administrative and supervisory
responsibilities including authority for making and
recommending policy decisions." Responsibilities include
"program planning, implementation, development and
evaluation; determining operations, activities and
organization of program;...preparing reports, proposals and
budget requests;...working with other community resources and
agencies to develop an effective and responsive program.
(see also declaration of Tony Leong, Jr., and 11-13-84 letter
of Floyd Wylie, attached).

Re: Tony Leong, Jr. and Conflict of Interest
December 17, 1984
page two

Dr. Leong is also the Executive Director of AJOB. He
works part-time after hours and receives a salary in excess
of $100.00 monthly. As Executive Director he prepares
proposals for funding, handles personnel matters and oversees
the budget of the community agency. He has been the
Executive Director of AJOB since its establishment in
December, 1974.

Among its funding sources, AJOB receives money from the
City of Berkeley for counseling, advocacy, job placement, job
development, English as a second language, and other services
for Asians. AJOB submits annual funding proposals to the
City of Berkeley. Proposals for certain types of funds (such
as Joint Training and Partnership Act [JPTA] and Community
Services Block Grant [CSBG]) are reviewed by the Health and
Human Services Department. Individual proposals are reviewed
by City staff members in the Department who have expertise in
the particular area covered by the proposal. After review,
the City Manager recommends an allocation of available grant
funds to the City Council. After the Council takes action,
department employees negotiate with representatives of AJOB
and other funded agencies to develop the final program and
prepare and execute a contract. Department staff reviews and
approves invoices for payment and conducts an annual
performance evaluation.

Dr. Leong's official responsibilities with the City are
not directly involved with funding. The applications for
grant funds are handled by another unit in the Department of
Health and Human Services. Nevertheless, it is alleged that
Dr. Leong's position is of a policy-making nature in that he
is involved in determining City policies and priorities for
mental health problems for youth which may include issues
related to job training for youth. Although a person in his
position might review a grant application for job training
funds for youth, he has not been asked to review such
proposals or any other funding proposals.

In previous years, Dr. Leong has prepared the AJOB
proposals for funds. He has advocated those proposals before
the City Council, City Manager and other City employees. He
negotiated the terms of the contract with City staff, signed
the contracts on behalf of AJOB and submitted AJOB's invoices
to the City for payment.

AJOB also receives Community Development Block Grant
funds. These funds are administered by the Planning &
Community Development Department, not the Health and Human

Re: Tony Leong, Jr. and Conflict of Interest
December 17, 1984
page three

Services Department. Dr. Leong does not have any
professional contact or interaction with City staff who
review CDBG proposals or administer the CDBG program. Dr.
Leong has taken the same actions with respect to preparing
AJOB's grant application and obtaining CDBG funds as are
outlined for JTPA and CSBC funds.

     AJOB also applied for funds which are not directly
administered by the City, but for which the City has certain
authority in a decision-making process concerning these
funds. For example, several months ago the City submitted a
grant proposal for state mental health funds. As supervisor
of the Family, Youth and Children's unit, Dr. Leong will be
responsible for administering these funds if they are
received. He worked with the Chief of the Mental Health
Division and the Mental Health Advisory Board to prepare the
City's application for grant funds. At the same time, AJOB
applied for the same funds to provide mental health
counseling services to the Asian adult immigrant population.
Although these funds are not administered by the City, the
City is required to sign off on applications submitted by
community agencies. Dr. Leong prepared and signed AJOB's
application and communicated directly with his supervisor,
Dr. Floyd Wylie, to obtain his approval of AJOB's application.

     Based on the facts outlined above, I would appreciate
your advice setting forth guidelines or standards of conduct
which Dr. Leong may use to avoid conflicts of interest in
these three situations:
     (1) Funds administered by the City of Berkeley:
          A. Grants processed by and through the Department
               of Health and Human Services.
          B. Grants processed by and through the Planning
               and Community Development Department.
     (2) Grants funded from sources outside the City in which
          the City approves or takes some other action regarding
          the application.

     As the City is presently holding up funds granted to
AJOB pending the resolution of this conflict of interest
issue, we would appreciate your response at your earliest
convenience.

Very truly yours,

ROBERT B. LEE
c/c: Natalie West, Berkeley City Attorney

sections.question:
Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant 
proposals are processed by the Health and Human Services 
Department? 
This question concerns Joint Trainining and 
Partnership Act and Community Services Block Grant funds. 
Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant 
proposals are processed by the Planning and Community 
Development Department? 
This question concerns Community 
Development Block Grant funds. 
Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grants administered by state agencies, but for which the City 
must approve the proposal or take some other action regarding 
the proposal? 


Robert B. Yee 
January 25, 1985
sections.conclusion: With regard to your first question, which concerns grant funds allocated pursuant to the Joint Training and Partnership Act and Community Block Services Grant programs and administered by the Health and Human Services Department, Dr. Leong must refrain from participating in the review or approval process for any grant proposals submitted by AJOB or submitted by other agencies competing with AJOB for those funds. Dr. Leong must also refrain from participating in the negotiation or administration of contracts between the City and AJOB in connection with those grant funds, on behalf of either the City or AJOB. Your second question concerns Community Development Block Grant funds allocated to AJOB by the Planning and Community Development Department. Dr. Leong, in his position with the Health and Human Services Department, has no involvement in this grant application review and approval process; therefore, Dr. Leong has no conflict of interest with respect to these grant proposals.

sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making or 
participating in a governmental decision in which he knows or 
has reason to know he has a financial interest. 
Section 87103 provides that an official has a financial 
interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it 
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public 
generally, on, among other interests, any source of income 
aggregating $250 or more received by or promised to the public 
official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is 
made. 
Dr. Leong receives a salary in excess of $100 per month 
from AJOBi therefore AJOB is a source of income in excess of 
$250 to Dr. Leong. 
The Commission, by regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702, has defined several ways in which a decision can have a 
material financial effect on a source of income. 
A decision is 
considered to have a "material" effect on a nonprofit 
organization if the decision's effect will be "significant." 
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section18702 (b) (3) (D) .) 
A determination as to 
whether an effect is "significant" can only be made in the 
context of a specific situation, after considering all the 
facts. 
A decision whether to make a grant to a nonprofit 
corporation is likely to be the type of decision which would 
have a significant effect on the nonprofit corporation. 
As 
stated before, whether a decision regarding a grant would have a 
significant effect on AJOB depends on the specific facts of the 
decision. 
However, it appears that, in most instances, 
Dr. Leong must refrain from making, participating in, or using 
his official position to influence, decisions regarding grants 
for AJOB.2/ 
In addition, under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 (b) (3) (B) , 
a decision has a material financial effect on an official's 
source of income when "there is a nexus between a governmental 
decision and the purpose for which the official receives 
income •••• " 
This regulation, in effect, prevents Dr. Leong from 
accomplishing as a City employee what he is paid to do as 
Executive Director of AJOB. 
~/ Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 
defines making, participating in, and using one's official 
position to influence a governmental decision (copy enclosed). 


Robert B. Yee 
January 25, 1985  
Since part of Dr. Leong's duties as Executive Director of 
AJOB is to prepare grant proposals and advocate the funding of 
those proposals before the City, there would be a nexus between 
Dr. Leong's position as Executive Director of AJOB and any 
actions Dr. Leong would take as a City employee which could 
foreseeably further the allocation of grant funds to AJOB. 
Accordingly, Dr. Leong, as an employee of the City, is required 
to refrain from making, participating in, or using his official 
position to influence, decisions which could foreseeably affect 
grant allocations to AJOB. 
Having outlined the general restrictions on Dr. Leong's 
participation in decisions affecting AJOB, I shall now apply 
these general principles to the specific facts presented. 
With regard to your first question, which concerns grant 
funds allocated pursuant to the Joint Training and Partnership 
Act and Community Block Services Grant programs and administered 
by the Health and Human Services Department, Dr. Leong must 
refrain from participating in the review or approval process for 
any grant proposals submitted by AJOB or submitted by other 
agencies competing with AJOB for those funds. 
Dr. Leong must 
also refrain from participating in the negotiation or 
administration of contracts between the City and AJOB in 
connection with those grant funds, on behalf of either the City 
or AJOB. 
You have stated that Dr. Leong's current 
responsibilities as Program Supervisor do not include review 
approval, negotiation, or administration of these contracts; 
however, I have included this information so that Dr. Leong 
would be aware of his responsibilities under the Political 
Reform Act in the event of a change in his duties as Program 
Supervisor. 
As Program Supervisor, Dr. Leong also participates in 
establishing policies and priorities for certain mental health 
problems which could include issues related to programs provided 
by AJOB. 
With respect to each of these decisions, Dr. Leong 
should consider whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the 
policy or priority decision will materially affect the 
allocation of grant funds to AJOB. 
For example, Dr. Leong must 
refrain from participating in any decision to discontinue City 
programs under which AJOB currently receives grant funds, or to 
lower the funding priority of those programs to the extent that 
it is likely that no funds, or a significantly reduced amount of 
funds, would be available. 
Your second question concerns Community Development Block 
Grant funds allocated to AJOB by the Planning and Community 
Development Department. 
Dr. Leong, in his position with the 


Robert B. Yee 
January 25, 1985  
Health and Human Services Department, has no involvement in this 
grant application review and approval process~ therefore, 
Dr. Leong has no conflict of interest with respect to these 
grant proposals. 
Your third question concerns grants from the state or other 
sources. 
Although the City does not administer these funds the 
City must approve or otherwise act on the proposals. 
Dr. Leong 
must refrain from participating in the City's actions on any 
grant proposals submitted by AJOB, and he must refrain from 
representing AJOB to the Department of Health and Human Services 
on these grant proposals. 
He must also refrain from 
participating in the City's actions on, or representing AJOB 
with regard to, any grant proposals which could foreseeably 
affect the allocation of grant funds to AJOB, such as proposals 
by other community agencies to provide similar services in the 
same area when there is a substantial likelihood that only one 
of these agencies will receive funding. 
You have stated that if the City receives state mental

Dr. Leong will administer those funds. 
It is not clear whether 
the programs proposed by the City could foreseeably affect 
AJOB's ability to receive funding for or to provide services. 
However, Dr. Leong must examine each decision regarding the 
administration of these City programs, and must refrain from 
participating in decisions which could foreseeably have a 
significant effect on AJOB's funding or services. 
My advice is confined to the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
You should contact the City Attorney's Office 
concerning the possible application of Section 1090, which is 
outside the Fair Political Practices Commission's jurisdiction.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Dr. Tony Leong, a city employee in the Berkeley Department of Health and Human Services who is also Executive Director of a nonprofit community agency (AJOB), has conflicts of interest regarding various grant funds that AJOB seeks from the city and state.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Dr. Leong must recuse himself from decisions regarding grants administered by the Health and Human Services Department to AJOB due to his financial interest as AJOB's Executive Director. However, he has no conflict regarding Community Development Block Grant funds since his department has no involvement in that process. The letter discusses state-funded grants but the conclusion section appears truncated.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document dated January 25, 1985 (File No. A-84-323). Contains complete FACTS and QUESTIONS sections with detailed DISCUSSION and partial CONCLUSION. Conclusion section appears truncated at end of provided text regarding state-funded grants. High confidence in extraction of first two questions and conclusions; third question conclusion is incomplete.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant 
proposals are processed by the Health and Human Services 
Department? 
This question concerns Joint Trainining and 
Partnership Act and Community Services Block Grant funds. 
Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grant funds administered by the City of Berkeley when the grant 
proposals are processed by the Planning and Community 
Development Department? 
This question concerns Community 
Development Block Grant funds. 
Does Dr. Leong have a conflict of interest with respect to 
grants administered by state agencies, but for which the City 
must approve the proposal or take some other action regarding 
the proposal? 


Robert B. Yee 
January 25, 1985

CONCLUSION: With regard to your first question, which concerns grant funds allocated pursuant to the Joint Training and Partnership Act and Community Block Services Grant programs and administered by the Health and Human Services Department, Dr. Leong must refrain from participating in the review or approval process for any grant proposals submitted by AJOB or submitted by other agencies competing with AJOB for those funds. Dr. Leong must also refrain from participating in the negotiation or administration of contracts between the City and AJOB in connection with those grant funds, on behalf of either the City or AJOB. Your second question concerns Community Development Block Grant funds allocated to AJOB by the Planning and Community Development Department. Dr. Leong, in his position with the Health and Human Services Department, has no involvement in this grant application review and approval process; therefore, Dr. Leong has no conflict of interest with respect to these grant proposals.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest (916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444 January 25, 1985 Robert B. Yee 1440 Broadway, Suite 810 Oakland, CA 94612 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-84-323 Dear Mr. Yee: Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Tony Leong concerning his duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ This advice is based on the facts provided in your letter. FACTS Dr. Leong is an employee of the Berkeley Department of Health and Human Services. Specifically, Dr. Leong is a Program Supervisor for the Family, Youth and Children's Program of the Mental Health Division of the Department of Health and Human Services. Dr. Leong's position of Program Supervisor is described in the City job announcement as having "extensive management, administrative and supervisory responsibilities, including authority for making and recommending policy decisions." Responsibilities include "program planning, implementation, development and evaluation; determining operations, activities, and organization of program . . . preparing reports, proposals and budget requests . . . working with other community resources and agencies to develop an effective and responsive program." Dr. Leong is also the Executive Director of Asians for Job Opportunities in Berkeley (AJOB), a nonprofit community agency which receives funding from the City of Berkeley, among other _______________ 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Robert B. Yee January 25, 1985 Page 2 sources, for counseling, advocacy, job placement, job development, English as a second language, and other services for Asians. Dr. Leong receives a salary from AJOB in excess of $100 per month. As Executive Director, he prepares proposals for funding, handles personnel matters, and oversees the budget of the community agency. AJOB submits annual funding proposals to the City of Berkeley. The City Health and Human Services Department reviews proposals for certain types of funds allocated to community agencies such as AJOB. Specifically, the Department reviews proposals for funds under the Joint Training and Partnership Act and Community Services Block Grant programs. When a community agency submits a proposal for funds, employees of the Health and Human Services Department with expertise in the area covered by the proposal review the proposal. After the review, the City Manager recommends to the City Council a decision on the allocation of grant funds to the community agency. If the City Council makes the allocation, Health and Human Services Department employees negotiate with the representatives of the funded agency to develop the final program and prepare and execute a contract. The Department staff also reviews and approves invoices for payment and conducts an annual performance evaluation. As Executive Director of AJOB, Dr. Leong prepares the AJOB proposals for funds, advocates these proposals before the City Council, City Manager, and other City employees,
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter on conflict of interest for Dr. Tony Leong, a Berkeley city employee who also serves as Executive Director of a nonprofit organization receiving city grants. The FPPC advises Dr. Leong must recuse himself from decisions affecting AJOB grants administered by his department but has no conflict regarding grants administered by other departments.
